title: 2016 Exhibit Case Venues
summary: A guide to the nine venues and seasonal exhibit rotations for local history displays throughout Brockport in 2016.

title: 2017 Exhibit Case Venues
summary: Details on the partnership between the museum and local businesses to showcase artifacts related to the Civil War and firefighting.

title: 2018 Exhibit Case Venues
summary: The 2018 venue list providing contact information and themes for exhibit cases located in public buildings and banks.

title: 2019-20 Museum Calendar
summary: Official schedule for the Board of Trustees, including meeting dates, officer nominations, and the annual picnic details.

title: Bridges and Trails (IP)
summary: Documentation of the interpretive panels installed along the Erie Canal, funded by the NYS Canal Corporation.

title: Helen Smagorinsky & Campus History
summary: Visual representations of Brockport’s history highlighting notable figures like Helen Smagorinsky and early street layouts.

title: Erie Canal Artifacts & Receipts
summary: A collection of 19th-century toll receipts, liftbridge diagrams, and postcards documenting Brockport's canal prosperity.

title: BCM Destinations Overview
summary: A summary of the 'Museum Without Walls' mission and a list of locations for interpretive panels throughout the community.

title: Outdoor Art Interpretive Panel
summary: Details regarding the dedication of the non-museum interpretive panel located in front of the Water Street Welcome Center.

title: Canal Commerce Panel
summary: A report on the Main Street bridge panel dedicated in 2011, documenting the commercial history of the Erie Canal.

title: Historic Downtown Churches
summary: An interpretive panel located in Sagawa Park highlighting the architectural and cultural history of Brockport's churches.

title: Civil War History in Brockport
summary: A comprehensive look at Civil War recruitment, casualty rates, and personal accounts from soldier John T. Farnham.

title: Erie Canal Kiosk Project
summary: A project report concerning the educational displays and kiosks installed for public viewing along the canal.

title: Early Farming & Farm Families
summary: Documentation of the farm history panel funded by the Town of Sweden Harvest Festival and dedicated in 2014.

title: Fire Department History Panel
summary: Information on the interpretive panel located at the Brockport Fire Department, dedicated to emergency service history.

title: Firefighting in Brockport Exhibit
summary: Exhibit catalog featuring scale models of 1930s fire trucks and historical firefighter badges from 1877 to 1972.

title: Friends of the Museum
summary: Recognition of community supporters, sponsors, and volunteers who contribute talent and collections to the museum.

title: Higher Education in Brockport
summary: A collaborative project with SUNY Brockport highlighting the history of the Normal School and early campus buildings.

title: J. Capen Hydrant History
summary: A unique panel mounted on a fire hydrant detailing its history at the Duffy-Mott company and local firefighting equipment.

title: 1979 Special Olympics Panel
summary: Commemoration of the Fifth International Special Olympics held at the SUNY Brockport campus.

title: Morgan-Manning House & History
summary: Documentation of the interpretive panel dedicated to the history and contributions of the Morgan family in Brockport.

title: Erie Canal Panel Redesign
summary: Details on the 2021 project to update and replace aging canal panels with new designs by Norm Frisch.

title: Main & Market Street History
summary: A panel funded by local 4th and 5th grade classes detailing the commercial history of the Main Street intersection.

title: Manufacturing in Brockport (Part 1)
summary: A collection of artifacts from local factories, including the Great Lakes Button Corp and Brockport Piano Co.

title: Manufacturing in Brockport (Part 2)
summary: Industrial history focusing on Owens-Illinois Glass, General Electric, and Black & Decker operations in Brockport.

title: Life of Mary Jane Holmes
summary: An exploration of the celebrity author's 40 novels and her status as a leading 19th-century woman novelist.

title: Updated Manufacturing Panels
summary: The 2021 replacement series for manufacturing interpretive panels along the Erie Canal trail.

title: A.D. Oliver School & Canal History
summary: Artifacts showcased at the middle school, including mule shoes and canal bridge diagrams from the early 1800s.

title: Postcards of Brockport (1910)
summary: A visual catalog of Brockport from a century ago, featuring the Monroe County Fair and historic local hotels.

title: The Protectives History Panel
summary: A historical panel commemorating the Protectives, Inc. No. 1 and their long-standing presence in the community.

title: Public Education in Brockport
summary: The museum's first interpretive panel (2006) focusing on the history of the A.D. Oliver school and local education.

title: Retail Trade & 20th Century Commerce
summary: An archive of commercial artifacts, trade cards, and merchandise sold in Brockport during the early 1900s.

title: Sagawa Park Murals
summary: Documentation of the mural panels dedicated in 2016 to provide historical context for the village park.

title: Museum Tour Map & Guide
summary: The master guide for the self-guided tour of Brockport’s 21 interpretive panels and 9 exhibit cases.

title: Venue & Theme Overview
summary: A high-level summary of museum partners and the nine major themes rotated through local exhibit cases.
